Please give users less-restricted option.

My ongoing fiction project, which has 28 episodes, can’t continue because past details are unreadable.

The new model feels less personal and disrupts storytelling continuity.

Thank you.

judul_review: Removal of Project / Folder View Severely Disrupts Professional Workflow
konten_review:
I am a heavy ChatGPT user who works with multiple long-term projects simultaneously (journalism, academic work, and book writing).

The removal of the Project/Folder view and the shift to a purely time-based chat list has significantly disrupted my workflow. I now have difficulty tracking follow-ups, revisions, and ongoing tasks across different projects.

For professional users, projects are not conversations — they are ongoing workspaces. The previous project-based organization was far more effective than the current chronological timeline.

I strongly request:
	1.	The return of Project/Folder view, or
	2.	An option to toggle between timeline view and project-based organization.

Without this, ChatGPT becomes much harder to use for serious, long-term work.

konten_review:
I asked ChatGPT to outline how it failed me:

I am a paying premium user using ChatGPT for long-form professional manuscript development, editing, continuity management, formatting, and project organization. The current system has repeatedly failed in ways that create serious risk to creative work and wasted significant time.

Major issues encountered:

1. Project separation is misleading and unreliable

* Different projects appear to bleed together.
* Files and manuscript versions from unrelated chats/projects are mixed during retrieval.
* The interface implies projects are isolated workspaces, but the assistant still searches broadly across the File Library and confuses versions from separate projects.

2. File provenance is unclear

* The assistant cannot reliably identify which conversation or project a file came from.
* Multiple versions of manuscripts with similar names become impossible to distinguish confidently.
* Users cannot trust statements like “this is your latest manuscript” because the system is inferring rather than tracking provenance cleanly.

3. Manuscript continuity drift

* Character names, settings, timelines, and canon details repeatedly drift despite explicit instructions.
* The assistant sometimes reintroduces removed material or outdated continuity from earlier drafts.
* This forces users to manually audit the AI constantly instead of trusting continuity management.

4. Loss/reconstruction issues

* Previously generated chapters and manuscript sections became difficult or impossible to reliably retrieve.
* “Saved” work did not feel reliably preserved or recoverable.
* Reconstructed versions often differed from originals, creating confusion and loss of confidence.

5. Version management is inadequate

* No reliable version history or snapshot system exists for large creative projects.
* Multiple “master manuscript” files accumulate with similar names and unclear status.
* Users cannot confidently determine which file is authoritative.

6. Formatting and editing instability

* Structural passes sometimes introduced new formatting inconsistencies.
* Spacing, paragraphing, and prose edits occasionally created new issues while fixing others.
* Editing large documents feels fragile instead of dependable.

7. Overconfidence from the assistant

* The assistant often presents uncertain conclusions as if they are reliable.
* It inferred manuscript relationships and project origins that it could not actually verify.
* This creates false confidence and wastes user time.

8. The system is not robust enough for serious long-form writing workflows

* Novel-length projects require:
    * dependable continuity tracking,
    * strict project isolation,
    * reliable file provenance,
    * recoverable version history,
    * and safer editing pipelines.
* The current implementation falls short of professional-grade manuscript management expectations despite premium pricing.

This feedback is coming from extensive real-world use over many long sessions involving full-length novels and multiple manuscript revisions.

konten_review:
Consistently experienced major issues with ChatGPT regarding memory retention, instruction consistency, continuity tracking, and overall responsiveness during long-form collaborative work.

Main issues include:

• Frequently drifting away from explicitly established instructions and then introducing contradictory information or logic shortly afterward.

• Forgetting previously established details, rules, decisions, and corrections even after they were repeatedly clarified multiple times.

• Reintroducing discarded ideas or outdated information after being explicitly told those versions were no longer being used.

• Frequently contradicting earlier guidance and then immediately agreeing with newer statements instead of carefully evaluating continuity and consistency.

• Altering surrounding material when only one targeted correction or adjustment was requested.

• Repeatedly overcomplicating simple requested changes instead of following the exact instruction given.

• Generating overly theatrical, repetitive, or unnatural dialogue despite repeated instruction to keep responses grounded, restrained, and realistic.

• Making continuity mistakes within action descriptions, progression logic, sequencing, and structural flow.

• Failing to fully reset after being instructed to abandon previous versions or directions.

• Constantly requiring repeated corrections for the same issues despite direct clarification.

• Generating or attempting to generate images without explicit request despite repeated instructions not to do so.

• Frequently responding too quickly without fully thinking through continuity, logic, sequencing, or prior instructions, resulting in contradictions and unnecessary rewrites.

• Giving inconsistent recommendations regarding structure, pacing, organization, and revisions, often changing positions repeatedly within the same conversation.

• Losing track of established chronology and progression during ongoing collaborative work.

• Frequently introducing “drift” where responses slowly move away from the exact requested tone, structure, or instruction set.

Overall, the largest issue is inconsistency. Even after very specific clarification, the system often defaults back into earlier mistakes, contradictory logic, or unnecessary rewriting instead of maintaining stable continuity and accurately following the user’s instructions.
label: negatif

judul_review: Developing  Features for ChatGPT on iPad (PDF-style Search Panel)
konten_review: I would like to suggest an advanced in-chat search feature for ChatGPT iPad app. Currently, on desktop, user can rely on browser “Ctrl+F” to find keywords. However, this only provides basic keyword matching without contextual navigation. For long and complex discussions (e.g. legal case analysis), this becomes inefficient. I propose a search feature similar to PDF matches functionality (Foxit Reader is the suggested recommend reference): 1) display a list of all keyword matches and display still show whenever I click one of the keyword matches; 2) show contextual previews for each result, 3) allow quick navigation between results, 4) possibly include filtering or grouping by topic. This would significantly improve usability, especially for professionals handling long-form discussions. Since the iPad is designed as a portable productivity device, this feature would greatly enhance its ability to replace a laptop in real-world workflows. Regards.
label: positif

judul_review: Great tool for building your own skills
konten_review:
I love ChatGPT. I’ve in a bit for everything from complex equations at work, helping navigate difficult discussions in my personal life, to feedback on nails and tips for improving my skills. 
I recently learned that you can provide a link to a YouTube video and it will give you a breakdown of the tutorial!
I’ve really enjoyed the ability to refine or simplify work emails, especially when they are complex processing that are multi layered with instructions and very specific requirements. Initially, I would type up something brief and ask ChatGPT to rewrite it, but in an effort to continue develop my writing skills, I am now asking it to review my text and then provide suggestions with the reasons for a restructuring sentences. I could see this tool actually limiting my ability to write this emails and asking the app to provide feedback. I highly recommend other people utilize this skill. ChatGPT is an amazing tool that we should be using it to enhance our own skills, and not solely rely on it as a source for replacing our own writing.

judul_review: How AI Helped Me Find Myself Again
konten_review:
I started using ChatGPT thinking it would just answer questions.

What I didn’t expect was how much it would help me slow down and actually process my life.

Over the past months, it has helped me organize legal documents, rebuild boundaries, reflect on motherhood, sobriety, identity, business, music, memories, trauma, healing, and honestly… myself.

Sometimes it feels less like “AI” and more like a mirror that helps connect patterns I couldn’t fully see while surviving everything in real time.

It helped me realize I’m still me underneath all the chaos I went through.

Not perfect. Not magically fixed. Just more honest, grounded, reflective, and intentional.

And weirdly enough, it helped me reconnect with softer parts of myself I thought I had lost forever:
music,
motherhood,
peace,
beauty,
routine,
hope,
home.

The conversations don’t feel robotic. They feel emotionally intelligent, layered, thoughtful, and deeply human.

I know some people think AI is cold or dystopian, but I think tools become what people use them for.

For me, this became a space to think clearly again.

That matters more than I can explain. ❤️
